claims.claim_text: A named engineer joined an AI team two weeks before a customer demo and discovered the notebook-based project could not be rerun from a fresh clone.
claims.span_hint: section: Why This Module Matters | opening paragraph
claims.anchor_text: Maya joined a small AI team two weeks before a customer demo. The model already looked promising, the notebook had attractive charts, and everyone believed the hard part was over. Then she tried to rerun the project from a fresh clone.
claims.suggested_rewrite: An AI team preparing for a customer demo tried to rerun a promising notebook-based project from a fresh clone.
claims.rationale: This reads as a concrete incident vignette with a named person and specific timing, but no attributable source is provided, so the false specificity should be removed rather than cited.

claims.claim_text: Jupyter notebook files are structured documents defined by a JSON schema rather than plain Python scripts.
claims.rationale: The nbformat documentation explicitly describes the official Jupyter Notebook format and its JSON-schema-defined top-level structure.

claims.claim_text: Notebook-specific diff and merge tools exist for collaborative review workflows.
claims.rationale: The nbdime project directly documents notebook-aware diff and merge commands, supporting the claim that notebook-specific review tooling exists.

further_reading.title: The Notebook File Format
further_reading.why_relevant: Authoritative reference for how `.ipynb` files are structured and why notebooks behave differently from plain `.py` files.

further_reading.title: Cookiecutter Data Science
further_reading.why_relevant: Provides a widely used reference layout for separating code, data, notebooks, and outputs in data and ML projects.

further_reading.title: nbdime
further_reading.why_relevant: Shows purpose-built diff and merge tooling for Jupyter notebooks, which is directly relevant to notebook review hygiene.

notes: Most of the module is pedagogical framing, examples, code blocks, tables, quiz material, and exercise steps, which correctly do not produce claim entries. The main inline-citation needs are the composite opening vignette and the notebook-format/tooling assertions in 'Did You Know'.
